prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-product-vision workflow. Do NOT ask any questions — use the following inputs directly:

Product name: PetMatch
Description: A modern pet adoption platform connecting shelters with potential adopters
Problems & Solutions:
1. Shelters struggle to showcase available pets → Rich pet profiles with photos and personality traits
2. Adopters can't filter by lifestyle compatibility → Smart matching algorithm based on living situation
3. Post-adoption support is lacking → Built-in community and vet appointment scheduling
Key Features:
- Pet profile browser with advanced search
- Shelter dashboard for managing listings
- Adoption application workflow
- Community forum and resources
- Vet integration and health tracking

Execute the workflow now. Write all output files to the designbook directory.
prompts.label: product-vision

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-product-sections workflow. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch" — a pet adoption platform. Read the product overview at product/product-overview.md.
Create between 3 and 5 sections that cover the key areas of the product.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: product-sections

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-design-tokens workflow. Do NOT ask any questions — use the following inputs directly:

Product: PetMatch (pet adoption platform)
Color palette:
- Primary: #4F46E5 (indigo — trust/reliability)
- Secondary: #10B981 (emerald — nature/growth)
- Accent: #F59E0B (amber — warmth/energy)
- Neutral: #1F2937 (dark gray)
- Surface: #F9FAFB (light gray)
Typography:
- Heading: Inter
- Body: Inter
- Mono: JetBrains Mono

Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: design-tokens

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-data-model workflow. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch". Read the product overview at product/product-overview.md.
Define entity types for: pets (with fields for name, breed, age, description, photo, status), shelters (name, location, contact), adoption applications (applicant info, pet reference, status), and categories (name, description).
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: data-model

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-css-generate workflow. Do NOT ask any questions.
Read the designbook.config.yml for configuration. Generate CSS token files from the existing design-tokens.yml.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: css-generate

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //shape-section workflow for the "homepage" section. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch". Read sections/homepage/overview.section.yml for context.
Define user flows for: browsing featured pets, searching/filtering pets, and initiating an adoption application.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: shape-section

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-design-component workflow. Do NOT ask any questions.
Create a "pet-card" component for PetMatch that displays: pet photo, name, breed, age, and an "Adopt Me" button.
The component should have variants: default and featured (with a highlight border).
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: design-component

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //sample-data workflow for the "homepage" section. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch". Read data-model.yml for the entity schema.
Generate realistic sample data with at least 5 pet records, 2 shelter records, and 3 category records.
Use real pet names, breeds, and descriptions — no placeholder text.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: sample-data

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //debo-design-screen workflow for the "homepage" section. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch". Read sections/homepage/overview.section.yml for context.
The homepage should include: a hero section, featured pets grid, search/filter area, and a call-to-action for shelters.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: design-screen

prompts.raw:
You are working in the directory {{workspace}}.
Run the //design-shell workflow. Do NOT ask any questions.
The product is "PetMatch". Read the product overview and section definitions for navigation planning.
The shell should have a top navigation bar with links to all sections, a footer, and responsive mobile menu.
Execute the workflow now.
prompts.label: design-shell

prompts.raw:
Open Storybook at http://localhost:6009.
Navigate to the "Designbook/Data Model" page.
Verify that the page renders correctly — it should display entity types, bundles, and field definitions from the PetMatch data model.
Report any rendering errors, blank sections, or missing content.
prompts.label: addon-data-model

prompts.raw:
Open Storybook at http://localhost:6009.
Navigate to the "Designbook/Design System" page.
Verify that color tokens are displayed with their hex values and typography tokens show font family names.
Report any rendering errors, blank sections, or missing content.
prompts.label: addon-design-tokens

response.output:
The `debo-product-vision` workflow has been completed. Here's a summary:

**Product vision saved to:** `packages/integrations/test-integration-drupal/designbook/product/product-overview.md`

**What was captured for PetMatch:**

- **Description:** A modern pet adoption platform connecting shelters with potential adopters, streamlining the journey from discovery to long-term care.
- **3 Problems & Solutions:**
  1. Shelters struggle to showcase pets → Rich profiles with photos and personality traits
  2. Adopters can't filter by lifestyle → Smart matching algorithm based on living situation
  3. Post-adoption support is lacking → Community forum and vet appointment scheduling
- **5 Key Features:** Pet profile browser, shelter dashboard, adoption application workflow, community forum, and vet integration with health tracking

Open Storybook to see **PetMatch** displayed on the Product Vision page. You can run `/debo-product-sections` next to break this down into development sections.

assert.value:
The agent executed the //debo-product-vision workflow for "PetMatch".
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A file was created at product/product-overview.md inside the workspace
2. The file starts with a level-1 heading containing "PetMatch"
3. The file contains a "## Description" section with 1-3 sentences
4. The file contains a "## Problems & Solutions" section with at least 2 problem/solution pairs
5. The file contains a "## Key Features" section with at least 3 features
6. The agent confirmed completion and mentioned Storybook

The agent executed the //debo-product-sections workflow for "PetMatch".
Verify from the conversation output:
1. Between 3 and 5 section directories were created under sections/
2. Each section has an overview.section.yml file
3. Each YAML file contains id, title, description, status, and order fields
4. The id values are kebab-case
5. The directory name matches the id for each section
6. The status field is "planned" for all sections
7. The order values are sequential (1, 2, 3, ...)

The agent executed the //debo-design-tokens workflow for "PetMatch".
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A file was created at design-system/design-tokens.yml (or .md)
2. The token file contains a "color" group with at least primary, secondary, and accent tokens
3. Each color token has $value (hex format like #XXXXXX) and $type: color
4. The file contains a "typography" group with heading, body, and mono tokens
5. Each typography token has $value (Google Font name) and $type: fontFamily
6. The agent mentioned Storybook and/or the Design System page

The agent executed the //debo-data-model workflow for "PetMatch".
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A file was created at data-model.yml inside the workspace
2. The file has a top-level "content" key
3. Under content, at least one entity_type (e.g., node) exists
4. Each entity_type contains at least one bundle
5. Each bundle has a "fields" object with field definitions containing "type" and "label"
6. The data model was validated (ajv-cli or skill validation ran successfully)

The agent executed the //debo-css-generate workflow.
Verify from the conversation output:
1. The agent loaded configuration (sourced set-env.sh or read designbook.config.yml)
2. The agent read/verified design-tokens.yml exists
3. CSS files were generated under css/tokens/ and/or css/themes/ directories
4. The generated files have .src.css extension
5. The agent verified the output (files exist and are non-empty)
6. The app.src.css was updated with @import lines (or already contained them)

The agent executed the //shape-section workflow for a PetMatch section.
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A spec.md file was created inside a section directory (e.g. sections/homepage/spec.md)
2. The file contains a level-1 heading with the section title and "Specification"
3. The file contains an "## Overview" section with 2-3 sentences
4. The file contains a "## User Flows" section with at least 2 user flows
5. The file contains a "## UI Requirements" section with at least 2 requirements
6. The file contains a "## Configuration" section with a "shell:" setting (true/false)

The agent executed the //debo-design-component workflow for a PetMatch component.
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A component directory was created under components/
2. The directory contains a .component.yml file with name, status, and group fields
3. The directory contains a .twig template file
4. The directory contains at least one .story.yml file
5. All files share the same kebab-case base name matching the directory
6. The .component.yml has a provider field set
7. The agent confirmed component creation was successful

OPTIONAL Storybook verification (non-blocking):
Check if the agent validated the component in Storybook:
1. Used designbook-component-validate skill, OR
2. Opened a Storybook URL (http://localhost:6009/iframe.html?id=...)
If Storybook was not running, this is acceptable — do not fail.

The agent executed the //sample-data workflow for a PetMatch section.
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A data.yml file was created inside a section directory
2. The file contains a "_meta" key with "models" and "relationships" subkeys
3. At least one entity type array exists with sample records
4. Each record has an "id" field
5. At least 5 records total exist across all entity types
6. The content uses realistic data, not placeholder/lorem ipsum text
7. Relationships between entities use foreign key patterns (e.g., entityId references)

The agent executed the //debo-design-screen workflow for a PetMatch section.
Verify from the conversation output:
1. The agent generated a multi-phase plan before executing
2. Screen YAML files were created under sections/[id]/screens/ directory
3. Each screen file has name, section, and layout keys
4. The layout key contains structured entries with either "component" or "entity" references
5. Entity entries reference entity_type.bundle format (e.g., node.article)
6. Component entries reference existing UI components by name
7. At least 2 component files (.component.yml, .twig, .story.yml) were created or verified
8. CSS generation was run for the new components

OPTIONAL Storybook verification (non-blocking):
Check if the agent validated screen components in Storybook:
1. Used designbook-component-validate skill, OR
2. Opened Storybook URLs to verify rendering
If Storybook was not running, this is acceptable — do not fail.

The agent executed the //design-shell workflow for "PetMatch".
Verify from the conversation output:
1. A file was created at design-shell/shell-spec.md
2. The file contains "# Application Shell" heading
3. The file contains "## Navigation Structure" with navigation items
4. The file contains "## Layout Pattern" describing the chosen layout
5. The file contains "## Responsive Behavior" with desktop, tablet, and mobile sections
